NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Product

Portable Evidence

Northroot records operational and agentic work as append-only evidence that can be copied, inspected, and verified offline.

Northroot is a trust kernel for portable evidence: append-only records, receipts, and offline verification for operational and agentic work.

The kernel is intentionally smaller than a runtime. It gives other systems a stable evidence shape without deciding how those systems plan, reconcile, approve, or execute work.

Evidence Shape

LayerPurpose
RecordsJSON or JSONL events describing what happened.
ReceiptsClaims bound to local evidence hashes.
JournalsOptional .nrj containers for framed append-only replay.
VerificationOffline checks that do not require a Northroot service.

Current Proof

The first proof is the GitHub repo inspection bundle. It records a read-only agentic work item with an actor, obligation, policy, event log, manifest, and receipt.

cargo run -p northroot -- verify examples/github-repo-inspection/receipt.json \
  --base-dir examples/github-repo-inspection \
  --json

The same event log can be replayed into a temporary .nrj journal and verified with the current CLI. The generated journal is a derived artifact, not the canonical source of the example.

Boundary

Portable evidence is not an operating console. It does not provide workflow state, hosted orchestration, reconciliation logic, sign-in, analytics, or approval UI. Those belong to runtimes and applications built around the kernel.

Operational Lessons

The evidence should remain useful after it leaves the system that produced it. That means stable hashes, explicit receipts, ordinary files first, and no hidden service dependency for verification.

Source

northroot/README.mdnorthroot/examples/github-repo-inspection/northroot/CORE_INVARIANTS.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
